features.name: Invoice number generation
features.description: Sequential invoice numbers in the format RE-{YYYY}-{NNNNN} scoped per org per calendar year, using Berlin time for year boundary.
risks: Race condition: two concurrent creates in the same org could receive the same number because gen_invoice_number counts then inserts non-atomically; the unique index on (org_id, number) would catch and reject the second write but no retry logic exists.

features.name: KVA/CE number generation
features.description: Sequential numbers per doc type and org per calendar year. Prefix map: kva→KVA, offer→ANG, order_confirmation→AB, invoice→RE. Counter counts ALL cost_estimates rows in the year regardless of type.
risks: Counter counts all cost_estimates rows (all types) for the year, so a KVA-2026-00003 may follow ANG-2026-00001 and ANG-2026-00002, creating numbering gaps within a single type. No uniqueness constraint exists on (org_id, number) for cost_estimates.

features.name: VAT-exclusive pricing
features.description: All prices entered and stored as net (exclusive of VAT). VAT is calculated per line item and summed separately. The gross total is net+vat. Subtotal column = net, vat_amount = sum of VAT, total = gross.

features.name: KVA to Invoice conversion
features.description: A cost estimate can be converted to an invoice. On conversion the source KVA is marked status='invoiced' and gets invoice_id set. The invoice inherits the KVA's line items, customer, and totals.
risks: Two writes (insert invoice + update KVA) are not in a DB transaction. Compensation logic (delete invoice if KVA update fails) is implemented but if that also fails an orphan invoice can exist with the source KVA still in its original status.

features.name: Agent KVA automation (hk_draftCostEstimate)
features.description: The ElevenLabs voice agent can draft a KVA via the hk_draftCostEstimate tool, gated on kva_enabled and kva_level>=2. At level 3 it also sends the KVA by email immediately.
risks: kva_automation_enabled (legacy) is still a fallback for kva_enabled — two toggles with slightly different semantics can confuse config.

features.name: Price list knowledge base sync (Preisauskunft)
features.description: When price_info_enabled is toggled on (or catalog items change), active priced catalog items are rendered into a German text document and attached to the ElevenLabs agent KB. Toggle off removes all Preisliste docs including orphans.
risks: Concurrent syncs for the same org can race (GET→PATCH is not serialized); acknowledged in code comments and tracked as a known issue.

features.name: PDF generation for KVA and invoices
features.description: A shared PDF renderer (build_pdf) handles all doc types (kva/offer/order_confirmation/invoice). Invoice PDFs include a bank-footer with IBAN/BIC/tax info; KVA PDFs include a legal disclaimer footer. Org logo is fetched and embedded (best-effort).
risks: Logo fetch has a 5-second timeout and is best-effort; a slow storage URL delays PDF generation for every request.

features.name: Catalog CSV import/export
features.description: Catalog items can be exported as semicolon-delimited CSV and bulk-imported. Import auto-detects delimiter (semicolon vs comma), rows missing a name are skipped, VAT defaults to 19% if absent, unit defaults to Stk. Import triggers KB sync as a background task.
risks: Import inserts all valid rows in a single batch; there is no deduplication — re-importing the same CSV creates duplicates.

features.name: Text modules for document templates
features.description: Orgs can define reusable text modules (intro, closing, payment terms, annotations) categorized by type. One module per category can be marked is_default and auto-populated into new KVA forms.

features.name: Auto-draft invoice on case completion (orphan feature)
features.description: maybe_create_invoice_for_project() is defined to auto-draft an invoice when a case is completed, gated on invoices_enabled + invoices_level>=2. The function exists but is NEVER called by any route or background task in the current codebase.
risks: This is dead code. The auto-invoice feature documented in the service comment (topic 19) is not wired to any trigger.

rules.name: Invoice number format and org-year scoping
rules.description: Invoice numbers follow the format RE-{YYYY}-{NNNNN} where YYYY is the current year in Europe/Berlin timezone and NNNNN is zero-padded to 5 digits. The sequence resets each calendar year and is scoped per org (count of org's invoices created >= Jan 1 of current year + 1).
rules.purpose: Unique, human-readable identifiers for German accounting compliance.
preconditions: Org exists and is not disabled
actions: Count invoices for org in current year
systemEffects: number field set on invoices row
failureConditions: Race condition: two concurrent creates may get same number; unique index (org_id, number) will reject the second with a DB error; no retry.
rules.evidence: def gen_invoice_number(client, org_id): year = now_berlin().year; res = client.table('invoices').select('id', count='exact').eq('org_id', org_id).gte('created_at', f'{year}-01-01').execute(); return f'RE-{year}-{(res.count or 0) + 1:05d}'

rules.name: KVA/CE number format with doc-type prefix
rules.description: Cost estimate numbers follow {PREFIX}-{YYYY}-{NNNNN} where PREFIX is KVA for kva, ANG for offer, AB for order_confirmation, RE for invoice type. The sequence counter counts ALL cost_estimates rows for the org in the current year (all types combined), so numbers are not sequential per type.
rules.purpose: Unique identifiers per document type for the org.
actions: Count all cost_estimates for org in current year
systemEffects: number field set on cost_estimates row
failureConditions: No uniqueness constraint on (org_id, number) for cost_estimates; duplicates theoretically possible under concurrent creates.
rules.evidence: prefix = {'kva': 'KVA', 'offer': 'ANG', 'order_confirmation': 'AB', 'invoice': 'RE'}.get(doc_type, 'KVA'); res = client.table('cost_estimates').select('id', count='exact').eq('org_id', org_id).gte('created_at', ...).execute(); return f'{prefix}-{year}-{(res.count or 0) + 1:05d}'

rules.name: VAT-exclusive line item pricing
rules.description: Line item net = quantity * price * (1 - discount_pct/100). VAT per line = line_net * vat_rate/100. Only items with kind=None, 'item', or 'optional' contribute to line_net; kind='text' and kind='subtotal' yield 0. Optional items are excluded from totals (shown on PDF as '-').
rules.purpose: German commercial practice: prices are net (netto), VAT shown separately.
inputs: positions[] with quantity, price, discount_pct, vat, kind
validations: kind must be one of: item, optional, subtotal, text (implicitly via if-guards)
actions: Sum net and VAT excluding optional/subtotal/text kinds
Apply total_discount_pct factor and add surcharge
rules.evidence: def compute_totals(positions, surcharge, total_discount_pct): ... for p in positions: if p.get('kind') not in (None, 'item'): continue; ln = _line_net(p); net_sum += ln; vat_sum += ln * float(p.get('vat') or 0) / 100; net = net_sum * factor + surcharge; vat = vat_sum * factor + surcharge * 0.19

rules.name: Invoice due date derivation
rules.description: Due date is computed as invoice_date + payment_terms_days calendar days. If invoice_date is absent or unparseable, due_date is null. Default payment_terms_days is 14. Due date is stored in the DB; it is not re-derived on read.
rules.purpose: Support standard German Zahlungsfrist (payment period) tracking.
systemEffects: due_date column written on invoices row
failureConditions: Unparseable invoice_date returns null due_date silently.
rules.evidence: def add_days(iso_date, days): d = date.fromisoformat(str(iso_date)[:10]); return (d + timedelta(days=int(days or 0))).isoformat()

rules.name: Invoice status lifecycle
rules.description: Stored statuses are: draft, sent, paid, cancelled. 'overdue' is a derived virtual status computed at list-read time for sent invoices whose due_date is before today (Berlin). It is never persisted. Status transitions have no enforcement (any stored status can be set to any other at any time via PATCH /status).
rules.purpose: Track invoice lifecycle for AR management and UI display.
validations: Status must be in {'draft', 'sent', 'paid', 'cancelled'} else HTTP 400
actions: Stamp timestamp column: paid→paid_at, cancelled→cancelled_at, sent→sent_at
systemEffects: status and relevant timestamp column updated on invoices row
failureConditions: Validation error if status not in STORABLE_STATUSES
rules.evidence: STORABLE_STATUSES = {'draft', 'sent', 'paid', 'cancelled'} / _STAMP = {'paid': 'paid_at', 'cancelled': 'cancelled_at', 'sent': 'sent_at'} / if r.get('status') == 'sent' and r.get('due_date') and str(r['due_date']) < today: r['status'] = 'overdue'

rules.name: Invoice delete restricted to draft status
rules.description: An invoice can only be deleted if its current status is 'draft'. Any other status (sent, paid, cancelled, overdue) returns HTTP 400 with German error message.
rules.purpose: Prevent deletion of issued or paid invoices to preserve audit trail.
preconditions: Invoice exists and belongs to org
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if not found
HTTP 400 with 'Nur Entwürfe können gelöscht werden.' if not draft
rules.evidence: if rows[0]['status'] != 'draft': return 'not_draft' / raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Nur Entwürfe können gelöscht werden.')

rules.name: Invoice send stamps sent status and sends email with PDF
rules.description: POST /send renders a PDF, sends it via the org's email provider, then stamps status='sent' and sent_at=now() only after a confirmed send. Email recipient: payload.to override → customer.email → HTTP 400. CC to org if copy_to_me=true. Email template precedence: request payload → email_configs.invoice_email_subject/body template → German hardcoded default.
rules.purpose: Deliver invoice to customer and record the send event.
validations: Recipient email must be resolvable (payload.to or customer.email); otherwise HTTP 400
actions: Send email with PDF attachment via send_email()
failureConditions: HTTP 400 if no recipient email
HTTP 502 if email send fails
rules.evidence: def _stamp(): client.table('invoices').update({'status': 'sent', 'sent_at': _now()}).eq('org_id', user.org_id).eq('id', inv_id).execute()

rules.name: Invoice duplication resets to draft with new number and today's date
rules.description: Duplicating an invoice creates a new row copying all fields except: id, number (regenerated), created_at, updated_at, sent_at, paid_at, cancelled_at, cost_estimate_id (cleared). Status resets to 'draft'. invoice_date resets to today (Berlin). due_date recalculated from new invoice_date + payment_terms_days.
rules.purpose: Allow quickly creating a similar new invoice from an existing one.
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if source not found
rules.evidence: for k in ('id', 'number', 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'sent_at', 'paid_at', 'cancelled_at', 'cost_estimate_id'): src.pop(k, None); src['status'] = 'draft'; src['invoice_date'] = today_iso(); src['due_date'] = add_days(src['invoice_date'], src.get('payment_terms_days'))

rules.name: KVA to Invoice conversion marks source as invoiced
rules.description: When an invoice is created with a kva_id (cost_estimate_id), the source cost estimate is updated to status='invoiced' and invoice_id=created_invoice_id. This is a two-step write without a transaction. Compensation: if the KVA update fails, the just-created invoice is deleted to avoid an orphan.
rules.purpose: Bidirectional link between KVA and invoice; prevent double-invoicing of same KVA.
preconditions: kva_id belongs to same org (validated via validate_fk_in_org)
validations: FK check: kva_id must belong to caller's org
failureConditions: If KVA update fails: compensate by deleting invoice; if invoice delete also fails: orphan invoice logged.
rules.evidence: Two writes, no transaction — if the back-link update fails, COMPENSATE by deleting the just-created invoice so we never leave an invoice whose KVA still shows 'draft' with no link (the conversion would look broken).

rules.name: Invoice case grouping via KVA inheritance
rules.description: When creating an invoice with a kva_id but no case_id, the system looks up the KVA's case_id. If the KVA has no case_id either, it falls back to the KVA's inquiry_id → inquiry.case_id. The resolved case_id is stored on the invoice.
rules.purpose: Keep invoices grouped under the correct Fall (case) for the cases view.
rules.trigger: POST /api/invoices with kva_id and no case_id
actions: If null, fetch KVA inquiry_id → fetch inquiry.case_id
rules.evidence: if row.get('cost_estimate_id') and not row.get('case_id'): kva = ...; cid = kva[0].get('case_id'); if not cid and kva[0].get('inquiry_id'): inq = ...; cid = inq[0].get('case_id') if inq else None; if cid: row['case_id'] = cid

rules.name: KVA status lifecycle with timestamps
rules.description: Valid cost estimate statuses: draft, sent, accepted, rejected, expired, invoiced. Transitions via PATCH /status stamp corresponding columns: sent→sent_at, accepted→accepted_at, rejected→rejected_at. draft and invoiced have no timestamp column. The POST /send route sets status='sent' and stamps sent_at directly.
rules.purpose: Track KVA negotiation state and enable AI follow-up suggestions (kva_followup requires non-null sent_at).
validations: DB constraint: status must be in (draft, sent, accepted, rejected, expired, invoiced)
failureConditions: DB constraint violation if invalid status supplied
rules.evidence: _STAMP = {'sent': 'sent_at', 'accepted': 'accepted_at', 'rejected': 'rejected_at'} — 'sent' added after regression where PATCH /status with 'sent' failed to stamp sent_at, causing kva_followup to never fire.

rules.name: KVA validity window
rules.description: Each KVA has a validity_days field (default 30) and a valid_until date computed as today+validity_days at creation time. This is stored and displayed; it is NOT used to auto-expire or change KVA status. PDF shows 'Gültig bis {date}' in the legal footer.
rules.purpose: Communicate offer validity period to customer; reference for §650c BGB disclaimer.
rules.evidence: valid_until_for(validity_days): return (now_berlin().date() + timedelta(days=int(validity_days or 30))).isoformat() — expiry is shown on PDF but no automated status transition to 'expired' is wired.

rules.name: Cross-tenant FK validation on invoice/KVA create and update
rules.description: Both invoice and cost estimate create and update routes validate all FK references (customer_id, kva_id/inquiry_id, case_id) using validate_fk_in_org(), which checks the referenced row exists in the caller's org. Rejects cross-tenant pointers with HTTP 422. Service-role client bypasses RLS so this is an explicit application-layer check.
rules.purpose: Prevent IDOR attacks: a user cannot link an invoice to another org's customer/KVA/case.
validations: validate_fk_in_org for each non-null FK: verify row exists with same org_id
failureConditions: FK points to row in another org: HTTP 422
rules.evidence: validate_fk_in_org(client, table='customers', fk_id=payload.customer_id, org_id=org_id, label='Kunde') / validate_fk_in_org(client, table='cost_estimates', fk_id=payload.kva_id, org_id=org_id, label='Kostenvoranschlag') / validate_fk_in_org(client, table='cases', fk_id=payload.case_id, org_id=org_id, label='Fall')

rules.name: Admin-only mutation, read allowed for all org members
rules.description: Create, update, delete, send, duplicate operations on invoices and cost estimates require org_admin role (require_org_admin). List, get, and PDF generation require any org member (require_org). Employees see invoices/KVAs as read-only; status change and delete buttons are hidden in the frontend.
rules.purpose: RBAC: employees cannot issue or modify financial documents.
validations: require_org_admin raises HTTP 403 for non-admin roles
rules.evidence: @router.post('') async def create_invoice(payload: InvoiceUpsert, user: CurrentUser = Depends(require_org_admin)) / {isAdmin && <>...mutations...</>}

rules.name: Agent KVA automation gating (kva_enabled + kva_level)
rules.description: The hk_draftCostEstimate agent tool is gated by two checks: (1) kva_enabled must be truthy (falls back to legacy kva_automation_enabled); (2) kva_level must be >= 2 (level 1 is blocked server-side per 2026-06-12 ruling). At level 2, KVA stays draft. At level 3, KVA is sent immediately via email if customer has a non-@temp.local email.
rules.purpose: Org-level automation control: prevent agent from creating KVAs without consent; L1 = agent never creates, L2 = team reviews drafts, L3 = agent sends immediately.
inputs: DraftCostEstimateRequest: customer_id, inquiry_id, subject, positions[], notes
validations: kva_enabled or kva_automation_enabled must be truthy
actions: If level=3 and customer has real email: send PDF email and stamp status='sent'
systemEffects: If L3+email: email sent, status updated to 'sent', sent_at stamped
failureConditions: Returns {success:false} if kva off or level<=1
Best-effort send: failure leaves as draft, never raises
rules.evidence: kva_on = cfg_row.get('kva_enabled'); if kva_on is None: kva_on = cfg_row.get('kva_automation_enabled'); if not kva_on: return {'success': False, ...} / if kva_level <= 1: return {'success': False, ...}

rules.name: KVA send stamps status and sends email with PDF
rules.description: POST /send on a cost estimate renders a PDF, sends via email, then stamps status='sent' and sent_at=now() only after confirmed send. On failure the status remains unchanged (stays draft). Template precedence: request payload subject/message → email_configs.kva_email_subject/body → German default. Supports copy_to_me flag for CC to org email.
rules.purpose: Deliver KVA/Angebot to customer and record delivery.
validations: Recipient email required (payload.to or customer.email); otherwise HTTP 400
failureConditions: HTTP 400 if no email
HTTP 502 if send fails; status unchanged on failure
rules.evidence: # Only stamp status after a successful send so failed-send retries stay in 'draft' instead of being misleadingly marked 'sent'.

rules.name: KVA binding vs unverbindlich and tolerance
rules.description: KVAs have an is_binding flag (default false). If non-binding, a tolerance_pct (default 20%) applies. PDF subtitle shows '(verbindlich)' or '(unverbindlich, Toleranz ±{tol}%)'. Legal footer cites §632 Abs. 3 BGB and §650c BGB for non-binding KVAs. If binding, footer says 'Vielen Dank für Ihr Vertrauen.' The tolerance_pct field is disabled in the form when is_binding=true.
rules.purpose: German legal compliance: distinguish binding offers from non-binding cost estimates under BGB.
actions: Use in PDF subtitle and legal footer
rules.evidence: if doc_type == 'kva': subtitle = '(verbindlich)' if binding else f'(unverbindlich, Toleranz ±{tol}%)' / legal = f'Dieser Kostenvoranschlag ist gemäß § 632 Abs. 3 BGB unverbindlich...'

rules.name: Invoice PDF includes bank footer; KVA PDF includes legal footer
rules.description: Invoice PDFs render a 3-column bank footer in the lower margin with: Bankverbindung (IBAN, BIC, bank_name, account_holder from org.bank_details), Geschäftsführung (managing_director), Steuernummer (vat_id, tax_number from org.tax_info). KVA PDFs use a text legal footer (BGB disclaimer). Invoice PDFs have a wider bottom margin (34mm vs 28mm).
rules.purpose: German invoice legal requirement to include payment and tax details.
actions: Render footer columns from org bank_details and tax_info
failureConditions: Missing bank/tax fields rendered as empty strings (no error)
rules.evidence: if doc_type == 'invoice': pdf.bank_footer = _invoice_bank_footer(org) / def _invoice_bank_footer(org): bd = org.get('bank_details') or {}; ti = org.get('tax_info') or {}

rules.name: Live PDF preview in form pages
rules.description: Both InvoiceFormPage and CostEstimateFormPage show a real-time PDF preview in a sidebar iframe. The preview is debounced 800ms after any form change, generated via POST /preview with the current form payload. The preview number is shown as 'VORSCHAU'.
rules.purpose: WYSIWYG editing: team sees the exact PDF output before saving.
rules.trigger: Any form field change in InvoiceFormPage or CostEstimateFormPage (debounced 800ms)
inputs: Full form payload including positions, texts, dates
actions: Set iframe src to new blob URL
failureConditions: Preview errors are silently ignored
rules.evidence: const handle = setTimeout(async () => { const url = await apiPostBlob('/api/invoices/preview', payload); ... setPreviewUrl(url) }, 800)

rules.name: KVA inherit case_id from linked inquiry
rules.description: When creating a cost estimate with inquiry_id but no case_id, the system looks up the inquiry's case_id and sets it on the KVA. This auto-groups the KVA under the correct Fall. Frontend shows a hint: 'Der KVA wird automatisch dem Fall dieser Anfrage zugeordnet.'
rules.purpose: Maintain case grouping without manual selection when linking a KVA to an inquiry.
rules.trigger: POST /api/cost-estimates with inquiry_id and no case_id
rules.evidence: if row.get('inquiry_id') and not row.get('case_id'): inq = client.table('inquiries').select('case_id').eq('org_id', org_id).eq('id', row['inquiry_id']).limit(1).execute().data; if inq and inq[0].get('case_id'): row['case_id'] = inq[0]['case_id']

rules.name: Catalog item org-scoping with RLS
rules.description: Catalog items belong to an org via org_id FK. RLS is enabled on the catalog_items table. The service-role client (bypass RLS) is used in routes but org_id is always set from the authenticated user's org_id. Items are ordered by name. Supplier is an FK to the customers table within the same org.
rules.purpose: Multi-tenant isolation: each org sees only its own catalog.
actions: Enrich with supplier_name from customers table
outputs: List of catalog items with supplier_name
rules.evidence: query = client.table('catalog_items').select(_COLS).eq('org_id', org_id) / alter table catalog_items enable row level security

rules.name: Catalog item defaults on create
rules.description: When creating a catalog item, missing fields are defaulted server-side: name→'Position', unit→'Stk', unit_price→0, is_active→True. No other server-side validation beyond schema type coercion.
rules.purpose: Ensure minimal valid state for a new catalog item.
actions: setdefault for name, unit, unit_price, is_active
rules.evidence: row.setdefault('name', 'Position'); row.setdefault('unit', 'Stk'); row.setdefault('unit_price', 0); row.setdefault('is_active', True)

rules.name: Catalog KB sync triggered on every catalog write
rules.description: After every catalog create, update, delete, or CSV import, sync_price_list_kb() is queued as a FastAPI BackgroundTask. This is best-effort: the sync never blocks or errors the catalog save.
rules.purpose: Keep the ElevenLabs agent's Preisliste knowledge-base doc in sync with the current catalog.
failureConditions: KB sync failure is logged but does not affect catalog save response
rules.evidence: background.add_task(sync_price_list_kb, user.org_id)

rules.name: Price list KB reconcile-by-name (Preisauskunft toggle)
rules.description: sync_price_list_kb() implements reconcile-by-name semantics: it removes ALL knowledge-base entries named 'Preisliste (Richtpreise)' from the ElevenLabs agent (including orphans whose doc_id the DB lost), then attaches exactly one fresh doc if price_info_enabled=true AND there are active items with unit_price>0. If price_info_enabled=false or no priced items: doc is removed. Ordering: create→PATCH agent→delete stale EL docs→update DB column.
rules.purpose: Self-healing sync: prevent orphaned price docs from causing the agent to quote prices after the toggle is turned off.
rules.trigger: sync_price_list_kb(org_id) called from catalog write background tasks or Preisauskunft toggle
actions: Split into Preisliste docs (by name) and others
If enabled+items: create new EL doc, desired=[others+new_doc]
failureConditions: If EL PATCH fails: new doc deleted, price_list_doc_id untouched (retryable)
No agent_id: returns {synced:false, reason:'no_agent'}
rules.evidence: stale_ids = [d.get('id') for d in kb if d.get('name') == DOC_NAME and d.get('id')] / desired = [d for d in kb if d.get('name') != DOC_NAME] / Ordering guarantees the DB never claims a state the agent doesn't have.

rules.name: Catalog quick-select in invoice and KVA forms
rules.description: Both InvoiceFormPage and CostEstimateFormPage offer a catalog quick-select dropdown showing active catalog items. Selecting an item fills description, price, and unit into a new or existing empty position. The dropdown uses /api/catalog?status=active.
rules.purpose: Speed up line item entry by reusing catalog data.
rules.trigger: User selects from catalog dropdown in form
actions: Fill position with catalog item name, unit_price, unit; use existing empty position or append new
outputs: Position row updated in form state
rules.evidence: const addCatalog = (catId) => { const c = catalog.find(x => x.id === catId); setPositions(ps => { const filled = newPos({description: c.name, price: c.unit_price, unit: c.unit || 'Stk'}) })

rules.name: KVA-to-invoice import in invoice form
rules.description: When navigating to /invoices/new?kva_id={id} (from 'In Rechnung umwandeln' button in CostEstimatesPage), the InvoiceFormPage auto-imports the KVA's positions, subject, and customer_id. This populates the form but does NOT create the invoice or mark the KVA as invoiced yet — that happens only on save.
rules.purpose: UX convenience: pre-fill invoice form from an agreed KVA.
rules.trigger: InvoiceFormPage mount with kva_id query param
actions: Set customerId, subject, positions from KVA data
systemEffects: Form state only; no DB write
failureConditions: Error toast if KVA fetch fails
rules.evidence: const kp = params.get('kva_id'); if (kp) { kvaParamHandled.current = true; importKva(kp) } / navigate(`/invoices/new?kva_id=${e.id}`)

rules.name: Auto-invoice on case completion (dead code / orphan)
rules.description: maybe_create_invoice_for_project() is implemented to auto-draft an invoice when a case is marked 'completed', gated on invoices_enabled and invoices_level>=2 in agent_configs. It creates one invoice per case, sourcing the most recently ACCEPTED KVA. After creation it marks the KVA as 'invoiced' and links it. However, this function is NEVER called anywhere in the current codebase — no route imports or invokes it.
rules.purpose: Described as 'topic 19' back-office automation: auto-invoice closed cases.
rules.trigger: NONE — function exists but has no callers
rules.evidence: grep -rn 'maybe_create_invoice_for_project' → only definition in invoices.py:44; zero import or call sites anywhere in the codebase. UNVERIFIED OBSERVATION: the function is unreachable.

rules.name: Text module default auto-population in new KVA forms
rules.description: When creating a new cost estimate, the frontend fetches GET /api/text-modules/defaults which returns one text module per category (first by sort_order with is_default=true). It pre-populates introText, closingText, and paymentTerms from 'einleitung', 'schlusstext', 'zahlungsbedingungen' categories. This also applies during agent live-fill via textDefaultsRef.
rules.purpose: Consistency: all KVAs start with the org's configured standard text.
preconditions: Text modules with is_default=true exist for the org
actions: Set form state for introText, closingText, paymentTerms if not already set
rules.evidence: if (textDefaults.einleitung) setIntroText(v => v || textDefaults.einleitung); if (textDefaults.schlusstext) setClosingText(...); if (textDefaults.zahlungsbedingungen) setPaymentTerms(...)

rules.name: KVA send email recipient: @temp.local addresses are skipped
rules.description: In the agent's L3 auto-send path (_send_draft_kva), email addresses ending in @temp.local are treated as placeholder addresses (never a real inbox) and skipped. The send is aborted and the KVA remains draft.
rules.purpose: Prevent sending KVA emails to internally-generated placeholder addresses that have no real inbox.
validations: to_email must be non-empty and not end with @temp.local
actions: Return False (no send) if address is placeholder
systemEffects: KVA stays draft; no email sent
rules.evidence: if not to_email or to_email.endswith('@temp.local'): return False

rules.name: Catalog CSV import deduplication absence
rules.description: POST /api/catalog/import inserts all valid rows in a single batch with no deduplication check. Re-importing the same CSV will create duplicate catalog items. Rows are skipped only if the name field is empty or missing.
rules.purpose: Bulk catalog population from external source.
inputs: Headers: Bezeichnung/Name, Artikelnummer, Beschreibung, Kategorie, Einheit, MwSt, Verkaufspreis, Einkaufspreis, Aktiv
validations: Rows with empty name are skipped
unit defaults to Stk if absent
rules.evidence: if rows: created = len(client.table('catalog_items').insert(rows).execute().data or []) — no upsert or deduplication, idempotency not guaranteed.

rules.name: Invoice overdue derived status on list read
rules.description: The GET /api/invoices list endpoint derives an 'overdue' status at read time for any invoice with status='sent' whose due_date is before today (compared as ISO strings). This virtual status is returned to the client but never persisted. The InvoicesPage front-end has 'overdue' in its STATUS_META for display and filtering.
rules.purpose: Surface overdue invoices for AR follow-up without requiring a scheduled job.
actions: For each sent invoice with due_date < today: set r['status']='overdue'
systemEffects: No DB write; status in API response only
outputs: Invoice list with derived overdue status
rules.evidence: if r.get('status') == 'sent' and r.get('due_date') and str(r['due_date']) < today: r['status'] = 'overdue'

rules.name: Org logo embedded in PDF (best-effort)
rules.description: PDF generation fetches the org's logo_url (Supabase Storage URL) over HTTP with a 5-second timeout, saves to a temp file, renders at 40mm wide at x=15, y=12, then deletes the temp file. Any failure (timeout, 404, render error) is silently swallowed — the PDF generates without the logo.
rules.purpose: Brand identity on customer-facing PDF documents.
rules.trigger: Any PDF generation call (invoice or KVA)
outputs: PDF with embedded logo (or without on failure)
failureConditions: Any exception silently swallowed
rules.evidence: except Exception: pass # PDF still generates without the logo / with urllib.request.urlopen(logo_url, timeout=5)

rules.name: Skonto (cash discount) stored but not applied to totals
rules.description: InvoiceUpsert accepts discount_pct (Skonto%) and discount_days (Skonto Tage) which are stored on the invoice. These are NOT applied to the computed subtotal/vat_amount/total — they are metadata for display only (shown on PDF as payment_terms_text if included there). The PDF template does not have a dedicated Skonto line.
rules.purpose: Record early-payment discount terms for customer reference.
rules.evidence: discount_pct: float | None = None # Skonto % / discount_days: int | None = None # Skonto Tage — stored but not included in compute_totals() call. UNVERIFIED OBSERVATION: not rendered in PDF Skonto line.
